title: Implementing Java Call-in
text: Implementing Java Call-in Configure Uniface so that it can generate Java wrapper files for Uniface components. For more information, see Configuring Java Call-In . Define Java call-in implementations for the Uniface components you want to call from Java. For more information, see Defining Java Implementations for Uniface Components . Generate the Java wrapper files and package them into a .jar file. For more information, see Generating and Compiling Java Wrapper Files and Generated Files for Java Call-In . Import the Uniface-generated wrapper files and the Uniface Java Call-In API into your Java development tool. In your Java development environment, create Java application sources that use the Uniface-supplied classes and APIs to instantiate Uniface components and activate operations on those components. Use the ConfigManagerBean to specify or look up the properties of the Uniface environment in which the Uniface component runs . For example, you can specify the Java VM as the Java call handler, specify the assignment and initialization files to use, or set command line switches. For more information, see Java Properties for Uniface Call-In . Use the methods available in the Java Call-in API to call operations on Uniface components, create and delete occurrences, retrieve and modify data, and so on. For more information, see Calling Uniface Components from Java . Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
